what_it_does: This file implements the routine firing path for the company runtime: it turns a stored Routine into an HTTP call to a local session supervisor, asks the supervisor to spawn a real Claude session with the routine's prompt, watches the session's event stream for the turn's done event, tears the session down (unless told to keep it), and returns a structured run record. It also provides a bounded goal-loop that re-fires a routine up to a hard max_rounds ceiling, evaluating each result against a substring or regex stop test.
observations: All supervisor communication uses urllib.request against DEFAULT_SUPERVISOR (env COMPANY_SUPERVISOR_BASE, default http://127.0.0.1:8771).
build_spawn_body is kept pure so it can be unit-tested without a real spawn.
fire() first hits /health and raises RoutineFireError if the supervisor is unreachable.
_capture_done opens /watch?session=<id> in a daemon thread and scans newline-delimited JSON events for type:done.
The run record includes routine_id, session id, claude_session_id, result, is_error, observed_done, and source.
run_goal_loop appends a truncated prior result to the prompt on subsequent rounds and enforces max_rounds >= 1.
done_when supports plain substring matching or /regex/ syntax.
The CLI main fires a routine by id and supports --keep.
standouts: Fail-loud design: unreachable supervisor or refused spawn raises RoutineFireError rather than returning a soft failure.
Bounded goal-loop with a hard max_rounds ceiling and mandatory evaluator/done_when.
Prompt injection is delegated to the supervisor's /spawn route by including prompt in the request body.
Watch-stream reader runs in a daemon thread with a join(timeout) stop pattern.
conventions: Heavy docstrings state intent and constraints up front.
Pure helper (build_spawn_body) separated from I/O for testability.
Environment variable with local default for service base URL.
Type hints are present but partial (e.g. routine_or_id is unhinted).
concerns: _capture_done's daemon thread may keep the HTTP connection open briefly after timeout because stop is set after join returns.
HTTP error bodies are assumed to be JSON; non-JSON error responses will crash json.loads.
is_error is set True when done is None, even though the spawn may have succeeded and the session is still running.
prompt_override uses truthiness, so an empty-string override is ignored.
No authentication, retry, or backoff on supervisor calls.
run_goal_loop builds the continuation prompt by string concatenation, which may be brittle for complex prompts.
notes: A thin, fail-loud orchestrator that delegates actual session spawning to the session supervisor and adds run records plus a bounded retry loop.
questions: What is the exact schema of the /watch done event returned by the supervisor?
What fields does the Routine dataclass/class expose beyond id, cwd, prompt, model, permission_mode, goal, done_when?
Is /watch a Server-Sent Events stream or plain newline-delimited JSON?
What values are valid for permission_mode?
How and where are per-turn costs captured if this file only returns a thin record?
Is routine_registry a function that returns a dict keyed by routine id?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a local-driven, thin orchestration over the session supervisor that produces durable run records and refuses silent failures.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make routine execution feel like a deterministic, record-producing operation rather than an ad-hoc shell invocation, while keeping all heavy spawn/transport logic inside the existing supervisor and adding explicit guardrails against silent failures and unbounded self-firing loops.
novelty: Distinctive elements include treating routine execution as a supervisor-orchestrated substrate, injecting the prompt through the /spawn body so the supervisor owns injection, and a built-in bounded goal-loop with substring/regex stop-test derivation from done_when.
contribution: A reusable routine-execution primitive: spawn-body construction, watch-stream capture, teardown coordination, structured run records, and a bounded goal-loop with an evaluator. A fused system would keep this orchestration surface and its fail-loud/ceilinged-loop semantics while connecting it to whatever registry and supervisor implementations exist.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that fires declared routines against a local session supervisor via HTTP endpoints (/spawn, /watch, /teardown), captures turn results into structured run records, and provides a bounded goal-loop with substring or regex stop tests.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a deterministic, fail-loud, thin orchestration layer for executing and re-executing routines through the session supervisor without duplicating spawn or transport logic.

symbols.does: Exception class raised when a routine cannot be fired.
symbols.description: A RuntimeError subclass used to make routine-fire failures explicit and teaching-loud, avoiding silent no-ops when the supervisor is unreachable or refuses a spawn.

symbols.does: Builds the JSON body for the supervisor's /spawn endpoint.
symbols.description: A pure helper that maps a Routine onto the /spawn request payload, including cwd, prompt, permission_mode, name, source, and optional model, so it can be unit-tested without performing an actual spawn.

symbols.does: Performs a JSON HTTP request against the supervisor.
symbols.description: A small internal wrapper around urllib.request that sends a method/path/body to the supervisor base URL and returns (status, parsed JSON), reading error bodies as JSON as well.

symbols.does: Reads a session's /watch stream until a done event arrives.
symbols.description: Opens the supervisor's /watch endpoint in a daemon thread, scans newline-delimited JSON events, and returns the first event whose type is done, or None if the timeout expires.

symbols.does: Inner thread target that consumes the watch stream.
symbols.description: The daemon-thread worker inside _capture_done that iterates over the HTTP response lines, parses JSON events, and exits when a done event is found or the stop event is set.

symbols.does: End-to-end routine fire: health check, spawn, watch, teardown, record.
symbols.description: Resolves the routine, verifies the supervisor is reachable, posts /spawn, captures the turn's done event, optionally tears down the session, and returns a structured run record; raises RoutineFireError on failure.

symbols.does: Derives a goal-loop stop test from a routine's done_when field.
symbols.description: Returns None if no done_when is set, otherwise returns a function that checks whether the result contains a literal substring or matches a /regex/ pattern.

symbols.does: Re-fires a routine up to max_rounds until an evaluator returns true.
symbols.description: A bounded goal-loop that fires the routine, evaluates the result, and re-fires with prior-result context if the goal is not met; it enforces a hard max_rounds ceiling and requires either an explicit evaluator or a routine done_when.
